title: Budget Frequency
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can regular budget reviews help you achieve your financial goals faster?
What review schedule balances effectiveness with practicality for your lifestyle?

stages.prompt: How often should you review your budget?

options.label: Once a year during annual planning
options.reflection: Annual reviews are too infrequent to catch spending issues and adjust to life changes.

options.label: Never - set it and forget it
options.reflection: Budgets need regular adjustment to remain relevant and effective for your financial goals.

options.label: Every day for maximum control
options.reflection: While thorough, daily budget reviews can become overwhelming and time-consuming.

options.label: Every month to stay current
options.reflection: Exactly! Monthly reviews keep your budget aligned with your actual spending and life circumstances.

stages.prompt: What's the best time to review your budget?

options.label: At the end of each month
options.reflection: Good timing! Reviewing at month-end helps you assess performance and plan for the next month.

options.label: Whenever you remember
options.reflection: Inconsistent timing makes it difficult to build budgeting habits and track progress effectively.

options.label: Beginning of each month for planning
options.reflection: Excellent! Starting each month with a budget review sets clear expectations and goals.

options.label: Only when you run out of money
options.reflection: Crisis-driven reviews are reactive rather than proactive and often too late to be helpful.

stages.prompt: What should you check during budget reviews?

options.label: Actual income vs projected income
options.reflection: Important baseline! Verifying income accuracy ensures your entire budget is built on solid foundations.

options.label: All spending categories for accuracy
options.reflection: Perfect! Comprehensive category review reveals where money actually goes versus where you planned.

options.label: What your friends are spending
options.reflection: Comparing to others' spending can lead to poor financial decisions and unnecessary pressure.

options.label: Nothing - trust your initial plan
options.reflection: Blindly trusting initial plans without review often leads to budget failure and financial stress.

stages.prompt: How should you adjust your budget after review?

options.label: Make small, incremental adjustments
options.reflection: Exactly! Small, thoughtful adjustments maintain budget stability while improving effectiveness.

options.label: Make major changes every month
options.reflection: Frequent drastic changes can create instability and make it hard to establish consistent habits.

options.label: Ignore discrepancies and hope for the best
options.reflection: Ignoring budget variances leads to continued overspending and missed financial opportunities.

options.label: Abandon the budget entirely
options.reflection: Giving up after setbacks prevents you from developing essential financial management skills.

stages.prompt: What's the benefit of consistent budget reviews?

options.label: Creates more financial stress
options.reflection: Actually, regular reviews reduce stress by preventing financial surprises and maintaining control.

options.label: Makes you feel more restricted
options.reflection: Reviews actually provide freedom by helping you understand your true financial capacity and choices.

options.label: Becomes boring routine work
options.reflection: When done thoughtfully, budget reviews become empowering tools for achieving financial goals.

options.label: Builds spending awareness and control
options.reflection: Perfect! Consistent reviews develop financial literacy and help you make intentional spending choices.
